637e8280dfdd9c1fe3b88cca.question: If the smallest edge of a Kepler triangle is one, then the hypotenuse equals the square of this number. A continued fraction comprised only of ones can be used to express this number. A regular pentagon whose side length is one will have diagonals with a length of this irrational number, which is also a positive solution to the equation x-squared minus x minus one equals zero. Consecutive numbers in the (*) Fibonacci sequence converge to this value, which is approximately 1.618 and symbolized phi. For 10 points, name this number that is equal to one plus the square root of five, all divided by two.
637e8280dfdd9c1fe3b88cca.question_sanitized: If the smallest edge of a Kepler triangle is one, then the hypotenuse equals the square of this number. A continued fraction comprised only of ones can be used to express this number. A regular pentagon whose side length is one will have diagonals with a length of this irrational number, which is also a positive solution to the equation x-squared minus x minus one equals zero. Consecutive numbers in the (*) Fibonacci sequence converge to this value, which is approximately 1.618 and symbolized phi. For 10 points, name this number that is equal to one plus the square root of five, all divided by two.
637e8280dfdd9c1fe3b88cca.answer: <u>golden ratio</u> [or <u>golden mean</u>; or <u>golden section</u>; or <u>golden proportion</u>; or <u>mean of</u> <u>Phidias</u>; accept <u>divine</u> in place of golden; accept <u>phi</u> or <u>numerical equivalents</u> before mentioned]
637e8280dfdd9c1fe3b88cca.answer_sanitized: golden ratio [or golden mean; or golden section; or golden proportion; or mean of Phidias; accept divine in place of golden; accept phi or numerical equivalents before mentioned]
